other thing a moment ago that my visual cortex is in this particular state is this a good way to characterize what colors are isn't this circular aren't you defining blue in terms of blue for me I think we should be cautious it's a little bit dangerous it's also a little bit dangerous to be a Mentalist our brains don't look to have colors colors don't look like they're in our heads in any way if they're in there then somehow we're experiencing the insides of our brains and the illimitable phew isn't any more compelling you open up your eyes every morning and there sure seem to be colors to be colors out there on things in the world so what is going on when we see these colors all day long none of these are particularly attractive I think if you reflect on them for a moment all of them reject the claim that colors are objective so what should we conclude yes there are reasons to believe that colors are subjective or mind dependent this alone isn't telling us much about what colors are and about how we perceive them so we have to do more than that all the known options for feeling in the story are odd but at least their theories are options that can be developed in questioned I'm not trying to push you into one of them or the other I'm trying to push you into accepting these basic claims yes there's reasons to reject the objectivity of color that alone isn't the end of the
